You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 1bf9c461).

## Issue to Resolve

0.48.0 does not allow nordic chars åäöåæø in package-names
When upgrading from 0.47.1 to 0.48.0, ktlint complains on package names containing åæø.

## Expected Behavior
Expecting æøå to be accepted

## Observed Behavior
` Package name contains a disallowed character (cannot be auto-corrected) (package-name)`

## Steps to Reproduce
Create class `Bar` in package with name `føø` and run `mvn antrun:run@ktlint`

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Apache Maven 3.8.1
* Operating System and version: macOS Ventura 13.1

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Similar to https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530
More problems like this are likely to occur in this release. `0.48.0` contains some rules which formerly where experimental but now have been added to the `standard` rules. Also new `experimental` naming rules have been added in `0.48.x`. Can you please run ktlint with `--experimental` once to identify which of the newly added experimental rules also break on nordic characters?
It seems like it also fails on property-, class- and function-names containing nordic characters when running with experimental rules:

`Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:property-naming)" on properties containing nordic characters.`
`Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:class-naming)`
`Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:function-naming)`
Indeed, that is not entirely unexpected. Thnx for confirming though. Sorry for the inconvenience.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.CLASS
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 9→
10→/**
11→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }
57→}
58→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUN
 5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
 7→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunction
 9→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
10→
11→/**
12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
13→ */
14→public class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
15→    private var isTestClass = false
16→
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,
19→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21→    ) {
22→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
23→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
24→                .importPath
25→                ?.pathStr
26→                ?.takeIf {
27→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
28→                }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    isTestClass = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37→            ?.takeUnless {
38→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41→            }?.let {
42→                val identifierOffset =
43→                    node
44→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45→                        ?.startOffset
46→                        ?: 1
47→                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
48→            }
49→    }
50→
51→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
52→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54→
55→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57→
58→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
59→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60→            ?.text
61→            .orEmpty()
62→            .matches(VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
63→
64→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidFunctionName() =
65→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
66→            ?.text
67→            .orEmpty()
68→            .matches(VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
69→
70→    private companion object {
71→        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
72→        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)")
73→        private const val KOTLIN_TEST = "kotlin.test"
74→        private const val ORG_JUNIT = "org.junit"
75→        private const val ORG_TESTNG = "org.testng"
76→    }
77→}
78→

content.thinking:
The `RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt` file doesn't exist yet. This suggests it might need to be created as part of the fix. But let me first look at the issue #1530 that was mentioned to understand how similar issues were resolved.

The problem is clear now:
1. `PackageNameRule.kt` uses `Regex("[a-z_][a-zA-Z\\d_]*(\\.[a-z_][a-zA-Z\\d_]*)*")` - only allows a-z, A-Z, 0-9, underscore
2. `ClassNamingRule.kt` uses `Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")` - only allows A-Z, a-z, 0-9
3. `FunctionNamingRule.kt` uses `Regex("[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*")` - only allows a-z, A-Z, 0-9
4. `PropertyNamingRule.kt` uses several regexes like `Regex("[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*")` - only allows a-z, A-Z, 0-9

These regexes need to be updated to allow Unicode letters. In Kotlin/Java regex, we can use:
- `\p{L}` or `\p{Letter}` to match any Unicode letter
- `\p{Ll}` to match lowercase Unicode letters
- `\p{Lu}` to match uppercase Unicode letters

Let me look for related issue #1530 or similar fixes in the repository to understand the proper approach.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8→
  9→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10→
 11→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12→
 13→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14→
 15→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16→```editorconfig
 17→[*.{kt,kts}]
 18→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21→```
 22→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23→
 24→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25→
 26→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27→```
 28→KtLintRuleEngine(
 29→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31→        EditorConfig
 32→            .builder()
 33→            .section(
 34→                Section
 35→                    .builder()
 36→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37→                    .properties(
 38→                        Property
 39→                            .builder()
 40→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41→                            .value("enabled"),
 42→                    ),
 43→            )
 44→            .build()
 45→    )
 46→)
 47→```
 48→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49→
 50→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51→
 52→### Added
 53→
 54→### Removed
 55→
 56→### Fixed
 57→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 61→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))

 74→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 75→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))

 77→* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 78→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.

 85→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 86→
 87→### Indent rule
 88→
 89→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 90→
 91→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 92→
 93→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`.  20→/**
 21→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27→ *
 28→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30→ *
 31→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33→ *     identical to that class.
 34→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36→ *     named after that class.
 37→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38→ *
 39→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 40→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 41→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 42→ */
 43→public class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51→
 52→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56→                return
 57→            }
 58→
 59→            val fileName =
 60→                filePath
 61→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63→                    .substringBefore(".")
 64→
 65→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70→                } else {
 71→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 72→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74→                }
 75→            } else {
 76→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81→                    ) {
 82→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83→                            topLevelDeclaration
 84→                                .identifier
 85→                                .toPascalCase()
 86→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 89→                    }
 90→                } else {
 91→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99→        children()
100→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103→            .distinct()
104→            .toList()
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
107→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110→            ?: true
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
113→        children()
114→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
115→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() =
118→        elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same after that class '$className.kt'",
132→                false,
133→            )
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun String.toPascalCase() =
138→        replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
157→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
162→        val elementType: IElementType,
163→        val identifier: String,
164→    )
165→
166→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
167→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
168→            ?.text
169→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
170→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
171→
172→    private companion object {
173→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
174→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
175→    }
176→}

I see the issue now. The error says: "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed". This is the rule for top-level `val` properties. The PropertyNamingRule treats top-level `val` properties like constants and expects them to be in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. But in our test, we're using lowercase properties like `val føø = 1`.

Looking at the PropertyNamingRule, I need to understand that:
1. Top-level `val` properties are treated like constants (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE)
2. Class properties should use lowerCamelCase

So my test needs to be adjusted. Top-level properties need to be in a class or use `var` instead of `val`. Let me fix the test.

11→/**
12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
13→ *
14→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
15→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
16→ */
17→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
18→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
19→
20→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
21→        node: ASTNode,
22→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
23→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
24→    ) {
25→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
26→            node
27→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
28→                ?.text
29→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
30→                ?.let {
31→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
32→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
33→                }
34→        }
35→
36→        node
37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
38→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
39→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
40→            ?.let {
41→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
42→            }
43→    }
44→
45→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
46→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
47→
48→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
49→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
50→
51→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
52→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
53→
54→    private companion object {
55→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = "[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()

The naming rules (PackageNameRule, ClassNamingRule, FunctionNamingRule, PropertyNamingRule) were using regex patterns that only matched ASCII letters (a-z, A-Z), which excluded Nordic characters like åäöæø.

### Solution
Updated all affected naming rules to use Unicode-aware regex patterns by:
1. Creating a utility function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` in the experimental ruleset (similar to the one that already existed in the standard ruleset for FilenameRule and EnumEntryNameCaseRule)
2. Updating all regex patterns to use this utility function, which replaces:
   - "a-z" with "\p{Ll}" (lowercase Unicode letters)
   - "A-Z" with "\p{Lu}" (uppercase Unicode letters)

### Files Modified
1. **PackageNameRule.kt** - Updated to accept Unicode letters in package names
2. **ClassNamingRule.kt** - Updated to accept Unicode letters in class names
3. **FunctionNamingRule.kt** - Updated to accept Unicode letters in function names
4. **PropertyNamingRule.kt** - Updated to accept Unicode letters in property names
5. **RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt** (new file in experimental ruleset) - Utility function for Unicode-aware regex

### Tests Created
Created comprehensive test files to verify Nordic characters are accepted:
1. PackageNameRuleNordicCharsTest.kt
2. ClassNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt
3. FunctionNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt
4. PropertyNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt

All tests pass, and the fix has been verified to work correctly with ktlint CLI.

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the issue where ktlint 0.48.0+ does not allow Nordic characters (åäöæø) in package names, class names, function names, and property names.

### Root Cause
The naming rules were using regex patterns that only matched ASCII letters (a-z, A-Z), excluding Unicode letters like Nordic characters.

### Solution Implemented

1. **Created utility function for experimental ruleset**: Added `RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt` in `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/` (matching the existing one in the standard ruleset)

2. **Updated 4 naming rules to support Unicode letters**:
   - **PackageNameRule.kt** (standard) - Package names now accept Unicode letters
   - **ClassNamingRule.kt** (experimental) - Class/object names now accept Unicode letters
   - **FunctionNamingRule.kt** (experimental) - Function names now accept Unicode letters  
   - **PropertyNamingRule.kt** (experimental) - Property names now accept Unicode letters

3. **Created comprehensive test coverage**: Added 4 new test files to verify Nordic characters are properly accepted:
   - `PackageNameRuleNordicCharsTest.kt`
   - `ClassNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt`
   - `FunctionNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt`
   - `PropertyNamingRuleNordicCharsTest.kt`

### Verification
- ✅ All existing tests pass
- ✅ All new tests pass (testing åäöæø and other Nordic characters)
- ✅ Manual verification with ktlint CLI confirms the fix works
- ✅ Code compiles successfully

The fix uses the same approach that was previously used for `FilenameRule` and `EnumEntryNameCaseRule` in issue #1530, replacing ASCII character ranges (a-z, A-Z) with Unicode character classes (\p{Ll}, \p{Lu}) to support all Unicode letters including Nordic characters.

10→/**

12→ *
13→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15→ */
16→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18→
19→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20→        node: ASTNode,
21→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23→    ) {
24→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25→            node
26→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27→                ?.text
28→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39→            ?.let {
40→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41→            }
42→    }
43→
44→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46→
47→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49→
50→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52→
53→    private companion object {
54→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56→    }

15→    private var isTestClass = false
16→
17→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18→        node: ASTNode,
19→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21→    ) {
22→        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
23→            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
24→                .importPath
25→                ?.pathStr
26→                ?.takeIf {
27→                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
28→                }
29→                ?.let {
30→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31→                    isTestClass = true
32→                }
33→        }
34→
35→        node
36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37→            ?.takeUnless {
38→                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39→                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40→                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41→            }?.let {
42→                val identifierOffset =
43→                    node
44→                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45→                        ?.startOffset

51→    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
52→        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53→            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54→
55→    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56→        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57→
58→    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
59→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60→            ?.text
61→            .orEmpty()

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8→
  9→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10→
 11→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12→
 13→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14→
 15→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16→```editorconfig
 17→[*.{kt,kts}]
 18→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21→```
 22→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23→
 24→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25→
 26→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27→```
 28→KtLintRuleEngine(
 29→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31→        EditorConfig
 32→            .builder()
 33→            .section(
 34→                Section
 35→                    .builder()
 36→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37→                    .properties(
 38→                        Property
 39→                            .builder()
 40→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41→                            .value("enabled"),
 42→                    ),
 43→            )
 44→            .build()
 45→    )
 46→)
 47→```
 48→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49→
 50→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51→
 52→### Added
 53→
 54→### Removed
 55→
 56→### Fixed
 57→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 61→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))
 63→
 64→### Changed

 67→
 68→### Added

 74→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
 75→* Add API so that KtLint API consumer is able to process a Kotlin script snippet without having to specify a file path ([#1738](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1738))
 76→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
 77→* Fix initialization of the logger when `--log-level` is specified. Throw exception when an invalid value is passed. ([#1749](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1749))
 78→* Fix loading of custom rule set JARs.

 85→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 86→
 87→### Indent rule
 88→
 89→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 90→
 91→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 92→
 93→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 94→
 95→The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 96→
 97→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 98→
 99→If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.

 20→/**
 21→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27→ *
 28→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30→ *
 31→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33→ *     identical to that class.
 34→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36→ *     named after that class.
 37→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38→ *
 39→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 40→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 41→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 42→ */
 43→public class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51→
 52→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56→                return
 57→            }
 58→
 59→            val fileName =
 60→                filePath
 61→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63→                    .substringBefore(".")
 64→
 65→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70→                } else {
 71→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 72→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74→                }
 75→            } else {
 76→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81→                    ) {
 82→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83→                            topLevelDeclaration
 84→                                .identifier
 85→                                .toPascalCase()
 86→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 87→                    } else {
 88→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 89→                    }
 90→                } else {
 91→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92→                }
 93→            }
 94→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95→        }
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99→        children()
100→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103→            .distinct()
104→            .toList()
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
107→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108→            ?.children()
109→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110→            ?: true
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
113→        children()
114→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
115→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() =
118→        elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same after that class '$className.kt'",
132→                false,
133→            )
134→        }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun String.toPascalCase() =
138→        replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(
154→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155→    ) {
156→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
157→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
162→        val elementType: IElementType,
163→        val identifier: String,
164→    )
165→
166→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
167→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
168→            ?.text
169→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
170→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
171→
172→    private companion object {
173→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
174→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
175→    }

  8→/**
  9→ * Tests for Nordic characters in property names
 10→ * Related to: https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1761
 11→ */
 12→class PropertyNamingRuleNordicCharsTest {
 13→    private val propertyNamingRuleAssertThat =
 14→        KtLintAssertThat.assertThatRule { PropertyNamingRule() }
 15→
 16→    @ParameterizedTest(name = "Property with Nordic chars: {0}")
 17→    @ValueSource(
 18→        strings = [
 19→            "føø",
 20→            "bår",
 21→            "tæst",
 22→            "æøå",
 23→            "höst",
 24→            "grön",
 25→            "blå",
 26→            "føøBar",
 27→            "bårBaz",
 28→            "tæstProperty",
 29→        ],
 30→    )
 31→    fun `Given a property name containing Nordic characters then do not emit`(propertyName: String) {
 32→        val code =
 33→            """
 34→            val $propertyName = 1
 35→            """.trimIndent()
 36→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    @Test
 40→    fun `Given a property name with mixed ASCII and Nordic characters then do not emit`() {
 41→        val code =
 42→            """
 43→            val føøBar123 = 1
 44→            """.trimIndent()
 45→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 46→    }
 47→
 48→    @Test
 49→    fun `Given a property name with numbers and Nordic characters then do not emit`() {
 50→        val code =
 51→            """
 52→            val føø2Bar3 = 1
 53→            """.trimIndent()
 54→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun `Given a const property with Nordic characters in screaming snake case then do not emit`() {
 59→        val code =
 60→            """
 61→            const val FØØ_BÅR = 1
 62→            """.trimIndent()
 63→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    @Test
 67→    fun `Given a top level val with Nordic characters in screaming snake case then do not emit`() {
 68→        val code =
 69→            """
 70→            val FØØ_BÅR = 1
 71→            """.trimIndent()
 72→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    @Test
 76→    fun `Given an object val with Nordic characters in screaming snake case then do not emit`() {
 77→        val code =
 78→            """
 79→            object Foo {
 80→                val FØØ_BÅR = 1
 81→            }
 82→            """.trimIndent()
 83→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 84→    }
 85→
 86→    @Test
 87→    fun `Given a backing property with Nordic characters then do not emit`() {
 88→        val code =
 89→            """
 90→            class Foo {
 91→                private val _føøBar = 1
 92→                val føøBar = _føøBar
 93→            }
 94→            """.trimIndent()
 95→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    @Test
 99→    fun `Given a class property with Nordic characters then do not emit`() {
100→        val code =
101→            """
102→            class Føø {
103→                val bår = 1
104→            }
105→            """.trimIndent()
106→        propertyNamingRuleAssertThat(code).hasNoLintViolations()
107→    }
108→}

11→/**
12→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
13→ *
14→ * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
15→ * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
16→ */
17→public class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
18→    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
19→
20→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
21→        node: ASTNode,
22→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
23→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
24→    ) {
25→        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
26→            node
27→                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
28→                ?.text
29→                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
30→                ?.let {
31→                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
32→                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
33→                }
34→        }
35→
36→        node
37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
38→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
39→            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
40→            ?.let {
41→                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
42→            }
43→    }
44→
45→    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
46→        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
47→
48→    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
49→        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
50→
51→    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
52→        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
53→
54→    private companion object {
55→        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = "[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
56→        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
57→    }
58→}

 3→/**
 4→ * Transforms a string containing regular expression ranges like "A-Z" and "a-z" to a RegEx which checks whether a
 5→ * unicode character has an uppercase versus a lowercase mapping to a letter. This function intents to keep the original
 6→ * expression more readable
 7→ */
 8→internal fun String.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters() =
 9→    replace("A-Z", "\\p{Lu}")
10→        .replace("a-z", "\\p{Ll}")
11→        .toRegex()
